amazon-athena.what: Serverless SQL queries directly on S3
trap: Use Parquet+partitions to cut cost 87%
Not for OLTP or real-time
Need Glue Crawler first for schema
amazon-athena.when: Ad-hoc SQL on S3 data, log analysis (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs)

aws-data-exchange.what: Subscribe to 3rd-party datasets, delivered to S3
trap: Not a migration tool — ongoing data subscription
aws-data-exchange.when: Need external financial, weather, geospatial data without building ingestion

key: Destinations: S3, Redshift, OpenSearch, Splunk, HTTP
trap: Near-real-time NOT real-time (buffered)
KDS=real-time; Firehose=delivery/load
amazon-data-firehose.when: Load streaming data into S3/Redshift/OpenSearch without code

amazon-emr.what: Managed Hadoop/Spark cluster for big data
trap: Use Spot for task nodes only — core nodes hold data
amazon-emr.when: Large-scale ETL, ML training, log analysis at petabyte scale

trap: Data Catalog integrates with Athena/Redshift Spectrum/EMR
Glue ≠ DMS — Glue is for analytics ETL, DMS for DB migration
aws-glue.when: Build data lake pipelines, convert CSV to Parquet, catalog S3 data

trap: KDS=real-time custom consumers; Firehose=managed delivery
1MB max record size
amazon-kinesis.when: Real-time clickstream, IoT telemetry, log ingestion

aws-lake-formation.what: Build and secure S3 data lakes with fine-grained access
key: Sits on top of S3 + Glue
trap: Replaces complex S3 bucket policy + IAM combos
Use when fine-grained data lake access control is needed
aws-lake-formation.when: Multiple teams/accounts need different access to same data lake

trap: MSK=bring existing Kafka workloads; KDS=AWS-native streaming
amazon-managed-streaming-for-apache-kafk.when: Migrate existing Kafka apps to AWS or need Kafka ecosystem tools

amazon-opensearch-service.what: Managed search and log analytics (Elasticsearch successor)
key: Ingest from Kinesis Firehose, CloudWatch, Logstash
trap: DynamoDB → Lambda → OpenSearch for searchable DynamoDB data
CloudWatch Logs → Subscription Filter → Lambda → OpenSearch
amazon-opensearch-service.when: Full-text search, log/event analytics, real-time monitoring dashboards

trap: QuickSight doesn't store data — queries sources directly
BI/visualization layer only
amazon-quicksuite.when: Dashboards and reports on top of Athena, Redshift, S3

key: Redshift Spectrum: query S3 without loading
trap: NOT for OLTP — use RDS
Based on PostgreSQL but not same feature set
amazon-redshift.when: Complex analytics on large structured datasets, BI workloads

amazon-eventbridge.what: Serverless event bus for event-driven apps
key: Only service with direct SaaS integrations
trap: Prefer over SNS for SaaS integrations
CloudWatch Events and EventBridge are same underlying API
amazon-eventbridge.when: Event-driven decoupling, react to AWS service events, SaaS integrations

key: Protocols: HTTP, Email, SMS, Lambda, SQS, Mobile
FIFO topics: ordered + dedup (SQS FIFO only)
trap: SNS=push; SQS=pull
Fan-out pattern: SNS → multiple SQS queues
amazon-simple-notification-service.when: Fan-out, alerts/notifications, trigger multiple consumers from one event

trap: Visibility timeout should be 6x Lambda timeout
FIFO max 300 TPS without batching
amazon-simple-queue-service.when: Decouple producers from consumers, absorb traffic spikes, async processing

trap: Don't chain Lambdas — use Step Functions for orchestration
Express max 5min; Standard for long-running
aws-step-functions.when: Multi-step workflows, human approval flows, complex error handling

aws-budgets.what: Set spend/usage alerts and automated actions
key: Types: Cost, Usage, Savings Plans, Reservation
Alert before or when threshold hit
Budget Actions: apply SCP/IAM or stop EC2/RDS
trap: Budgets=proactive alerts; Cost Explorer=historical analysis
aws-budgets.when: Get notified before overspending, auto-stop resources at budget limit

trap: CUR=most detailed; Cost Explorer=visualization/forecasting
aws-cost-and-usage-report.when: Detailed cost allocation, chargeback, custom billing analysis

trap: Cost Explorer=analysis; Budgets=alerts; CUR=raw data
aws-cost-explorer.when: Understand spending patterns, get RI/SP recommendations, forecast costs

key: User Data: bootstrap script, runs once at first launch
Hibernate: saves RAM to EBS, max 60 days
trap: Spot=2min warning before interruption
Never put access keys on EC2 — use IAM role
User Data runs as root, once only
amazon-ec2.when: Need full OS control, persistent long-running workloads, GPU instances

aws-elastic-beanstalk.what: PaaS — deploy apps without managing infra
trap: Immutable=safest; All-at-once=fastest with downtime
RDS inside EB env is deleted with env — use external RDS for prod
aws-elastic-beanstalk.when: Quick app deployment, dev/test, teams without infra expertise

aws-lambda.what: Serverless functions, run code on demand
key: VPC: needs NAT GW for internet access
trap: Lambda in VPC loses internet — add NAT GW
SQS visibility timeout = 6x Lambda timeout
API GW hard limit 29s
aws-lambda.when: Event-driven code <15min, APIs, file processing, scheduled tasks

aws-batch.what: Managed batch computing at any scale
key: Job Queue → Job Definition → Compute Environment
trap: Batch=any runtime, long jobs; Lambda=short <15min event-driven
aws-batch.when: Genomics, rendering, financial modeling, ML training — long batch jobs

aws-app-runner.what: Deploy containers/source code as web apps instantly
trap: Not for complex microservices — use ECS/EKS for that
aws-app-runner.when: Quickly deploy simple APIs or web apps from container/code without K8s expertise

key: Image scanning: CVE detection (Basic or Inspector)
trap: Always pair with ECS/EKS for deployments
amazon-elastic-container-registry.when: Store and manage Docker images for ECS/EKS deployments

key: Launch types: EC2 (you manage) or Fargate (serverless)
Task Definition: blueprint (image, CPU, memory, ports, role)
Task Role vs Instance Profile — use Task Role per task
ALB dynamic port mapping for microservices
trap: Task Role=per-task permissions; Instance Profile=EC2-level
EC2 launch type needs ECS Agent installed
amazon-elastic-container-service.when: Run containers on AWS, prefer AWS-native over Kubernetes

key: Node types: Managed Groups / Self-managed / Fargate
IRSA: IAM roles per pod via service accounts
Add-ons: CoreDNS, kube-proxy, VPC CNI, EBS CSI
trap: ECS=AWS-native; EKS=Kubernetes-compatible — choose EKS for existing K8s workloads
amazon-elastic-kubernetes-service.when: Already using Kubernetes, need K8s ecosystem tools

trap: Cannot use GPU or some specialized instance types — use EC2 launch type then
Each task is isolated compute environment
aws-fargate.when: Containers without managing servers, variable workloads, serverless containers

trap: Global DB=active-passive (one writer region)
Aurora ≠ RDS — Aurora is AWS-built, not just managed
amazon-aurora.when: Best relational DB performance on AWS, MySQL/PostgreSQL, multi-region DR

trap: Not actual MongoDB — uses Aurora storage tech
DocumentDB=MongoDB-compat; DynamoDB=AWS-native NoSQL
amazon-documentdb.when: Migrate existing MongoDB workloads to AWS

trap: Scan=reads entire table (expensive) — always use Query
On-Demand=unpredictable; Provisioned+ASG=steady/cheaper
FIFO Streams have ordering guarantee
amazon-dynamodb.when: Any scale NoSQL, serverless apps, gaming, session stores, real-time leaderboards

key: Redis: Multi-AZ, persistence, pub/sub, sorted sets, backup
Memcached: multi-threaded, no persistence, simple horizontal scale
Lazy loading vs Write-through caching strategies
trap: Redis=HA+persistence+complex data; Memcached=simple cache only
ElastiCache cannot cache DynamoDB — use DAX for that
amazon-elasticache.when: Reduce DB read load, session storage, leaderboards, rate limiting

key: Engines: MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, Aurora
Read Replicas: async, up to 5, cross-region
RDS Proxy: connection pooling for Lambda/ECS
trap: Multi-AZ=HA not read scaling
Read Replicas=read scaling not HA
RDS Proxy=connection pooling not a load balancer
amazon-rds.when: Standard relational DB needs, OLTP, structured data with SQL

trap: NOT for OLTP
Redshift ≠ Athena — Redshift loads data; Athena queries S3 in place
amazon-redshift.when: Data warehouse, complex analytics on structured data

aws-database-migration-service.what: Migrate databases to AWS with minimal downtime
key: Heterogeneous: different engine → use SCT first
CDC: continuous replication after initial load
trap: Heterogeneous migration needs AWS SCT for schema conversion first
DMS=DB migration; Glue=analytics ETL
aws-database-migration-service.when: Migrate Oracle/SQL Server to Aurora/RDS with minimal downtime

trap: CodePipeline=orchestration; CodeBuild=build; CodeDeploy=deploy — glues them together
aws-codepipeline.when: Automate release pipeline from commit to deployment

trap: No servers to manage — auto-scales
Use SSM/Secrets Manager for secrets in buildspec
aws-codebuild.when: Build, test, produce artifacts in CI pipeline

aws-codedeploy.what: Automate code deployments to EC2, Lambda, ECS
trap: CodeDeploy does NOT provision infra — deploys to existing targets
Blue/Green on Lambda uses traffic shifting (canary/linear)
aws-codedeploy.when: Automate deployments with rollback, zero-downtime blue/green

trap: X-Ray Daemon must run alongside app (sidecar on ECS)
Sampling reduces cost but may miss some traces
aws-x-ray.when: Debug latency, trace requests across Lambda/API GW/ECS/EC2

aws-cloud-development-kit.what: Define infra in TypeScript/Python/Java/Go — compiles to CloudFormation
key: cdk synth → cdk diff → cdk deploy
trap: CDK generates CloudFormation — all CFN limits apply
Not a separate deployment system
aws-cloud-development-kit.when: Infrastructure as real code, reuse patterns as libraries

key: Pre-installed: AWS CLI, Python, Node, git
trap: Uses console session permissions — not a separate IAM user
aws-cloudshell.when: Quick CLI commands without local setup

aws-command-line-interface.what: Command-line tool to manage AWS services
key: Uses Access Key ID + Secret Access Key
trap: Never store access keys on EC2 — use IAM Instance Role
aws configure stores creds in ~/.aws/credentials
aws-command-line-interface.when: Automate AWS operations, scripting, local development

trap: Prevents fetching from internet on every build — saves time and improves security
aws-codeartifact.when: Centralize and secure package dependencies for builds

trap: AWS announced end of new customer onboarding 2024 — exam may still include it
aws-codecommit.when: Private Git hosting fully within AWS (legacy/existing setups)

key: Auto-provisions: Cognito, AppSync, DynamoDB, Lambda, S3
trap: Amplify=fastest full-stack serverless; not for fine-grained infra control
aws-amplify.when: Quickly build + host full-stack serverless web/mobile apps

aws-appsync.what: Managed GraphQL API with real-time subscriptions
key: Sources: DynamoDB, Lambda, RDS, OpenSearch, HTTP
Auth: API Key / IAM / Cognito / OIDC / Lambda
trap: AppSync=GraphQL+real-time; API Gateway=REST/HTTP/WebSocket
Subscriptions need WebSocket support on client
aws-appsync.when: Real-time apps, aggregate multiple data sources in one query, offline-capable mobile

key: Detects: entities, sentiment, key phrases, language, PII
Comprehend Medical: PHI in clinical text
trap: Comprehend=NLP/text analysis; Kendra=document search; Textract=document extraction
amazon-comprehend.when: Analyze customer feedback, classify tickets, detect PII in text

key: Returns precise answers not just links
Indexes: S3, SharePoint, Salesforce, OneDrive, DBs
trap: Kendra=smart search; Comprehend=text analysis — don't confuse
amazon-kendra.when: Internal document search, knowledge base Q&A

amazon-lex.what: Build chatbots and voice interfaces (Alexa tech)
key: Deploy to web, Slack, Twilio, Facebook
trap: Lex=chatbot builder; Polly=TTS; Transcribe=STT
amazon-lex.when: Customer service bots, voice ordering systems, FAQ automation

trap: Polly=TTS output; Transcribe=STT input; Lex=conversation

key: Detects: objects, faces, text, celebs, unsafe content
Custom Labels: train for your objects
trap: Rekognition=image/video analysis; Textract=document text extraction
amazon-rekognition.when: Identity verification, content moderation, media tagging

trap: SageMaker=full ML lifecycle; Rekognition/Comprehend/Polly=pre-built AI — no ML expertise needed for pre-built
amazon-sagemaker-ai.when: Custom ML models: build, train, tune, deploy at scale

amazon-textract.what: Extract text + structured data from documents
trap: Textract=document data extraction; Rekognition=image analysis
amazon-textract.when: Automate invoice/form processing, extract data from scanned docs

trap: Transcribe=STT; Polly=TTS; Lex=conversation/chatbot
amazon-transcribe.when: Meeting transcription, call analytics, subtitle generation

trap: Translate=translation; Comprehend=language detection+analysis
amazon-translate.when: Localize apps, translate user content, multilingual chatbots

amazon-cloudwatch.what: Monitoring, logs, and alerting for AWS
key: Custom Metrics: push RAM, disk via PutMetricData
Logs: groups→streams; Insights for queries; Subscriptions for streaming
trap: CloudWatch=what's happening now; CloudTrail=who did what; Config=what changed
EC2 RAM/disk NOT in default metrics — must use custom metrics
amazon-cloudwatch.when: Monitor AWS resources, set alerts, centralize logs

key: Stack: group of resources as one unit
trap: CloudFormation is free — pay for resources only
CDK generates CloudFormation under the hood

aws-cloudtrail.what: API audit log — who did what and when
key: Organization Trail: all accounts in one
trap: CloudTrail=audit; CloudWatch=monitoring; Config=compliance
Disabled CloudTrail is a security finding
aws-cloudtrail.when: Audit API calls, compliance, forensics, detect unauthorized changes

aws-config.what: Track and evaluate resource configuration compliance
key: Config Rules: AWS managed (150+) or custom Lambda
trap: Config=configuration compliance; CloudTrail=API audit
Config does NOT prevent changes — only evaluates after
aws-config.when: Check if resources are always configured correctly, compliance auditing

key: SCPs: restrict permissions on member accounts
SCPs cannot grant permissions — only restrict
Management account NOT restricted by SCPs
trap: SCPs don't apply to management account
SCPs are AND with IAM — most restrictive wins
aws-organizations.when: Multi-account strategy, billing consolidation, security guardrails at scale

aws-systems-manager.what: Operational management for EC2 + on-prem servers
key: Session Manager: SSH/RDP with no port 22/key pairs
Run Command: execute scripts at scale
SSM Agent: pre-installed on Amazon Linux 2
trap: Session Manager=preferred secure shell (no bastion needed)
Parameter Store=lightweight config/secrets; Secrets Manager=auto-rotation
aws-systems-manager.when: Secure shell access, fleet management, config storage, patching

trap: Trusted Advisor=proactive checks; Config=ongoing compliance; Security Hub=finding aggregation
aws-trusted-advisor.when: Quick health check, find idle resources, security gaps, service limit warnings

key: Generates risk report with improvement recommendations
Custom Lenses for your own questions
trap: Know all 6 pillars — exam scenarios often map to a specific pillar
aws-well-architected-tool.when: Architecture review, identify risks before launch, compliance prep

aws-cloudshell.what: Browser shell with pre-auth AWS CLI
trap: Uses console session IAM permissions
aws-cloudshell.when: Quick CLI commands without local setup

aws-health-dashboard.what: Personalized AWS service health events for your account
key: PHD: events impacting YOUR specific resources
trap: PHD=your account's specific impact; public dashboard=overall AWS status
aws-health-dashboard.when: Get alerted about AWS maintenance/outages affecting your resources

aws-license-manager.what: Manage BYOL software licenses (Microsoft, Oracle, SAP)
key: Set rules to prevent license violations
trap: License Manager=governance for vendor licenses on AWS/on-prem
aws-license-manager.when: Prevent license over-usage, compliance for expensive enterprise software

amazon-kinesis-video-streams.what: Ingest, store and process video streams from devices
key: Consumers: Rekognition Video, SageMaker, custom ML
trap: KVS=video/media only; KDS=generic data records
amazon-kinesis-video-streams.when: Smart cameras, industrial video monitoring, video ML pipelines

key: Agentless (VMware OVA): inventory + performance, no agents
Agent-based: detailed process + network dependency data
trap: ADS=discovery BEFORE migration; MGN=actual migration tool
aws-application-discovery-service.when: First step of migration: map what you have and how services connect

key: Supports: physical, VMware, Hyper-V, Azure, GCP → EC2
trap: MGN=lift-and-shift as-is; DMS=database migration/re-platform
Does NOT modernize or refactor
aws-application-migration-service.when: Migrate servers to EC2 with minimal downtime and no app changes

aws-datasync.what: Fast automated data transfer to/from AWS
key: Sources: NFS, SMB, HDFS, S3-compatible, AWS-to-AWS
trap: DataSync=bulk migration/periodic sync; Storage Gateway=ongoing hybrid access
DataSync=online (network); Snowball=offline (physical)
aws-datasync.when: Migrate NFS/SMB to EFS/S3, scheduled nightly sync, AWS-to-AWS transfer

aws-transfer-family.what: Managed SFTP/FTP/FTPS/AS2 endpoints backed by S3/EFS
trap: Transfer Family=ongoing file exchange protocol gateway; DataSync=bulk data transfer
AS2=EDI/B2B transactions (healthcare, finance)
aws-transfer-family.when: Partners/clients use SFTP today and you want to move files to S3 without changing their tooling

amazon-api-gateway.what: Managed API front door (REST, HTTP, WebSocket)
trap: REST API≠HTTP API — REST has caching/WAF/usage plans; HTTP is cheaper/simpler
29s timeout for sync Lambda calls
CORS must be enabled on API GW for browser calls
amazon-api-gateway.when: Expose Lambda/services as HTTP APIs, manage traffic, auth, throttling

amazon-cloudfront.what: CDN — cache content at 400+ global edge locations
key: OAC: restrict S3 to CloudFront only (replaces OAI)
Lambda@Edge: Node/Python up to 30s at edge
trap: CloudFront cert MUST be in us-east-1
CloudFront≠Global Accelerator — CF caches; GA routes without caching
EC2 origin needs public IP or ALB in front
amazon-cloudfront.when: Global static/dynamic content delivery, S3 website CDN, DDoS protection

amazon-route-53.what: Scalable DNS + domain registration + health checks
key: Alias: free, works at zone apex, points to AWS resources
Private Hosted Zones: DNS inside VPC
trap: CNAME can't be at zone apex — use Alias
Geolocation≠Latency: geo=where you are; latency=fastest response
Multi-value≠load balancer — just DNS with health checks
amazon-route-53.when: DNS routing, failover, weighted traffic splitting, health-checked DNS

elastic-load-balancing.what: Distribute traffic across targets in multiple AZs
key: ALB (L7): path/host/header routing, Lambda targets, Cognito auth, dynamic port mapping
NLB (L4): millions RPS, static IP, preserves client IP, ultra-low latency
GWLB (L3): route to firewall appliances, GENEVE port 6081
Cross-zone LB: default on ALB (free), off on NLB (charged)
trap: ALB doesn't preserve client IP — use X-Forwarded-For header
NLB does preserve client IP — SG must allow actual client IPs
ALB can't handle pure TCP/UDP — use NLB
elastic-load-balancing.when: ALB=web/microservices HTTP; NLB=high-perf TCP/UDP/gaming; GWLB=security appliances

aws-direct-connect.what: Dedicated private network connection to AWS
key: VIF types: Private (VPC) / Public (AWS services) / Transit (TGW)
Direct Connect Gateway: access VPCs in multiple regions
trap: DX is NOT encrypted — use VPN over DX for encryption
DX takes weeks — use VPN for quick connectivity
DX Gateway≠Transit Gateway
aws-direct-connect.when: Consistent low-latency/high-bandwidth to AWS, hybrid cloud, large data transfer

aws-global-accelerator.what: Route traffic over AWS backbone for global performance
key: 2 static Anycast IPs as global entry points
Endpoints: ALB, NLB, EC2, EIP in any region
trap: GA≠CloudFront: GA routes TCP/UDP, no caching; CF caches HTTP content
Use GA for non-HTTP or when you need static IPs + instant failover
aws-global-accelerator.when: Global TCP/UDP apps, gaming, IoT, instant failover, static IP requirement

aws-transit-gateway.what: Hub-and-spoke network connector for VPCs + on-prem
key: ECMP: aggregate VPN bandwidth across tunnels
trap: VPC Peering is NOT transitive — use TGW for transitive routing
TGW costs per attachment + per GB — VPC Peering has no per-hour cost
aws-transit-gateway.when: Connect many VPCs, replace complex peering mesh, on-prem + multi-VPC hub

amazon-cognito.what: Identity for app users (not AWS users)
key: Identity Pools: exchange token → temp AWS creds via STS
trap: User Pools=authentication only (JWTs)
Identity Pools=AWS authorization (temp creds)
Cognito=app users; IAM=AWS users
amazon-cognito.when: App login, social sign-in, give mobile users access to AWS resources

amazon-detective.what: Investigate root cause of security incidents
key: Uses GuardDuty + CloudTrail + VPC Flow Logs + EKS logs
trap: GuardDuty=alarm; Security Hub=dashboard; Detective=forensics/investigation

key: Analyzes: CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, DNS, EKS, RDS, S3 events
Malware Protection: scan EBS on compromised EC2
trap: GuardDuty DETECTS but does NOT block — use Lambda+SG/NACL/WAF to block
Disabling GuardDuty deletes all findings — use suppression rules instead
amazon-guardduty.when: Continuous threat detection across all accounts

key: Targets: EC2 (needs SSM Agent), ECR images, Lambda
trap: Inspector=vulnerability scanning (what could be exploited)
GuardDuty=threat detection (what is happening)
Macie=sensitive data in S3
amazon-inspector.when: Find CVEs on EC2/containers/Lambda, network reachability checks

amazon-macie.what: ML-powered sensitive data discovery in S3
key: Finds: PII, financial data, credentials, PHI
trap: Macie=S3 only — not EBS, RDS, EFS, DynamoDB
amazon-macie.when: Detect PII/sensitive data in S3, data privacy compliance

key: Externalize authz logic from app code
Works with Cognito for auth context
trap: Verified Permissions=your app's users; IAM=AWS resource access
amazon-verified-permissions.when: App-level RBAC/ABAC, policy changes without code deploys

aws-artifact.what: Download AWS compliance reports (SOC, PCI, ISO)
key: Reports: SOC1/2/3, PCI DSS, ISO, FedRAMP, HIPAA BAA
trap: Artifact=AWS's own compliance docs; Audit Manager=YOUR compliance evidence
aws-artifact.when: Auditors need AWS compliance certifications, sign HIPAA BAA

aws-audit-manager.what: Automate evidence collection for compliance audits
key: Pre-built frameworks: PCI, GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, CIS
Auto-collects evidence from CloudTrail, Config, Security Hub
trap: Audit Manager=YOUR evidence; Artifact=AWS's certifications
aws-audit-manager.when: Preparing for SOC2/PCI/HIPAA audit, continuous compliance monitoring

aws-certificate-manager.what: Free managed SSL/TLS certificates for AWS services
key: Works with: ALB, CloudFront, API GW, Beanstalk
trap: CloudFront certs MUST be in us-east-1
Does NOT work directly on EC2 — needs ALB/NLB in front
aws-certificate-manager.when: HTTPS on ALB, CloudFront, API Gateway — no manual cert management

key: Managed Microsoft AD: full AD, MFA, trust with on-prem
AD Connector: proxy to on-prem AD (no AWS storage)
Simple AD: Samba4, basic, cheap, no trusts/MFA
trap: AD Connector fails if VPN/DX to on-prem goes down
Simple AD has no MFA or trust relationships
aws-directory-service.when: EC2 domain join, WorkSpaces, apps needing Windows AD authentication

key: Policy types: Identity / Resource-based / Permission Boundary / SCP / Session
Permission Boundary: caps max permissions (doesn't grant)
Never use root for daily tasks
trap: SCPs don't apply to management account
Permission Boundaries don't grant — they limit max
Groups cannot assume roles — users and services can

aws-iam-identity-center.what: SSO for all AWS accounts + cloud apps
key: Permission Sets → IAM roles in each account
Identity sources: built-in, AD, Okta, Azure AD (SAML)
trap: Identity Center=workforce AWS access; Cognito=app end-users
aws-iam-identity-center.when: Multi-account SSO, centralize user access, federate corporate IdP

key: CMK types: AWS Owned (free) / AWS Managed ($0/key, auto-rotate) / Customer Managed ($1/mo)
Envelope Encryption: for data >4KB — data key encrypts data, KMS encrypts data key
Key Policy required on every key (must allow root)
Multi-Region Keys: same material in multiple regions
trap: Key deletion=irreversible after 7-30d waiting period
KMS=multi-tenant; CloudHSM=dedicated single-tenant hardware
Key Policies must explicitly allow root — unlike IAM
aws-key-management-service.when: Encrypt EBS/S3/RDS/etc., manage key rotation, audit encryption usage

aws-network-firewall.what: Managed stateful L3-L7 VPC firewall + IDS/IPS
trap: Network Firewall=VPC-level any traffic; WAF=HTTP only for ALB/CF/API GW
Requires route table changes to redirect traffic through firewall endpoints
aws-network-firewall.when: Block outbound to malicious domains, IDS/IPS for VPC traffic, centralized egress inspection

aws-resource-access-manager.what: Share AWS resources across accounts without duplication
key: Share: VPC subnets, TGW, Route 53 rules, License configs, Aurora, ACM Private CA
VPC Subnet sharing: multiple accounts deploy into same VPC
No VPC peering needed for shared subnets
trap: RAM=share resources; not for sharing data (use S3 bucket policies for that)
aws-resource-access-manager.when: Multiple accounts sharing same VPC, TGW, or Route 53 rules

aws-secrets-manager.what: Store and auto-rotate secrets (DB creds, API keys)
key: Native rotation: RDS, Aurora, Redshift, DocumentDB
trap: 'Automatic rotation of DB credentials' → always Secrets Manager
SSM Parameter Store=cheaper, no auto-rotation
Secrets Manager > SSM when rotation is needed
aws-secrets-manager.when: Database passwords, API keys — especially when auto-rotation is required

aws-security-hub.what: Single pane of glass for security findings
key: Aggregates: GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, Config, IAM Analyzer, Firewall Manager
Evaluates against CIS / FSBP / PCI standards
trap: Security Hub=aggregation dashboard; GuardDuty=detection; Detective=investigation
Requires Config enabled for many checks
aws-security-hub.when: Centralize all security findings, compliance scoring, automated response

key: Advanced: $3K/month, DRT 24/7, financial protection, L7 with WAF
Advanced covers: EC2, ELB, CloudFront, Route53, Global Accelerator
trap: Shield Standard=free automatic; Advanced=paid enterprise-grade
Shield Standard does NOT protect L7 — add WAF for that
aws-shield.when: Standard=everyone (free); Advanced=critical apps needing DRT + cost protection

aws-waf.what: Web Application Firewall for HTTP/S traffic
key: Deploy on: ALB, CloudFront, API GW, AppSync, Cognito
Rules: IP sets, SQLi, XSS, geo, rate-based, regex, managed groups
Firewall Manager: enforce WAF across all accounts
trap: WAF=L7 HTTP protection; Shield=volumetric DDoS; Network Firewall=any VPC traffic DPI
WAF on CloudFront=global; WAF on ALB=regional — separate Web ACLs
aws-waf.when: Block SQLi/XSS, rate limit, geo-block, OWASP Top 10 protection

key: REST: full features (cache, WAF, usage plans)
HTTP: cheaper, simpler, JWT native auth
VPC Link: connect to private ALB/NLB
trap: REST≠HTTP API feature set
29s Lambda sync timeout
CORS must be enabled on GW not just Lambda
amazon-api-gateway.when: Expose Lambda as HTTPS API, manage throttling/auth/caching

trap: AppSync=GraphQL+real-time; API GW=REST/HTTP
aws-appsync.when: Real-time apps, mobile offline sync, aggregate multiple data sources

aws-lambda.what: Serverless functions — run code without servers
trap: VPC Lambda loses internet — add NAT GW
SQS visibility timeout=6x Lambda timeout
API GW 29s sync limit
aws-lambda.when: Event-driven <15min tasks, APIs, file processing, triggers

trap: Don't chain Lambdas calling each other — use Step Functions
Express=5min max; Standard=long-running
aws-step-functions.when: Multi-step workflows, orchestrate services, human approvals, complex error handling

amazon-elastic-block-store.what: Persistent block storage for EC2 (single AZ)
key: gp3: 3K IOPS + 125MB/s baseline, max 16K IOPS — preferred over gp2
Multi-Attach: io1/io2 only, same AZ, up to 16 instances
trap: EBS is AZ-locked — snapshot to move to another AZ/region
st1/sc1 cannot be boot volumes
Unencrypted→encrypted: snapshot copy with encryption
amazon-elastic-block-store.when: EC2 root/data volumes, databases, single-instance persistent storage

key: Performance modes: General Purpose (default) / Max I/O (set at creation, can't change)
trap: EFS Linux only — FSx for Windows for SMB
Performance mode can't change after creation
Standard-IA has retrieval fee — if accessed often, Standard is cheaper
amazon-elastic-file-system.when: Shared file storage across multiple EC2/ECS/EKS, content management, home dirs

key: OAC: restrict bucket to CloudFront only
Object Lock WORM: Governance or Compliance mode
trap: CRR+SRR need versioning on BOTH buckets
Standard-IA/One Zone-IA: min 30d + 128KB charge
SSE-KMS has KMS API rate limits at high throughput
amazon-s3.when: Any object storage: backups, data lake, static web hosting, archives

key: Vault Lock: WORM compliance, immutable once locked
trap: Flexible≠instant — plan retrieval time
Min storage duration charged even if deleted early
Vault Lock≠Object Lock — Vault Lock for Glacier direct API
amazon-s3-glacier.when: 7-10 year compliance archives, infrequently accessed data, lowest cost storage

key: Covers: EC2, EBS, RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB, EFS, FSx, S3, Storage Gateway
Backup Vault Lock: WORM, even root can't delete
trap: Backup Vault Lock has 72h cool-off before becoming immutable
aws-backup.when: Single backup strategy across all services, compliance, cross-account DR

aws-storage-gateway.what: Hybrid cloud: connect on-prem to AWS storage
key: S3 File Gateway: NFS/SMB → S3 objects + local cache
FSx File Gateway: SMB → FSx for Windows + local cache
Volume Cached: primary in S3, hot data cached on-prem (iSCSI)
Volume Stored: primary on-prem, async backup to S3 (iSCSI)
Tape Gateway: virtual tape library → S3/Glacier
trap: Storage Gateway=ongoing hybrid; DataSync=bulk migration
File Gateway=file protocol; Volume Gateway=block (iSCSI)
aws-storage-gateway.when: Extend on-prem storage to cloud, replace tape, hybrid file shares

key: CIDR: /16 to /28; 5 reserved IPs per subnet
Public subnet: route to IGW + public IP
trap: 5 IPs reserved per subnet (not 4)
Cannot change VPC CIDR after creation — add secondary CIDRs

key: IGW: attach to VPC, add route 0.0.0.0/0 → IGW in public subnet
NAT GW: in PUBLIC subnet, private subnet routes 0.0.0.0/0 → NAT GW
NAT GW: 5-100 Gbps, charged per hour + GB
One NAT GW per AZ for HA
trap: NAT GW is in PUBLIC subnet not private
Single NAT GW=single AZ failure point
NAT GW=outbound only, not inbound
internet-gateway-and-nat.when: IGW=public internet access; NAT GW=private instances need outbound internet (updates, APIs)

key: SG: instance-level, stateful, allow-only, evaluates all rules
NACL: subnet-level, stateless, allow+deny, rule number order (lowest first)
Custom NACL: denies all by default
Ephemeral ports 1024-65535 needed in NACL outbound
trap: SGs stateful=return traffic auto-allowed
NACLs stateless=must allow both inbound AND outbound explicitly
SGs can't deny — use NACL to block specific IPs
security-groups-and-nacls.when: Use both for defense-in-depth; NACL for IP blocking; SG for fine-grained instance access

vpc-endpoints.what: Private access to AWS services without internet
key: Gateway: S3 + DynamoDB ONLY, FREE, via route table
Interface (PrivateLink): all other services, charged per hour + GB, creates ENI
Interface can be accessed from on-prem; Gateway cannot
trap: Gateway=free S3/DynamoDB only; Interface=everything else (charged)
Gateway Endpoints can't be accessed from on-prem — use Interface for that
vpc-endpoints.when: Private EC2 needs S3 without NAT (Gateway); SSM/Secrets Manager without internet (Interface)

vpc-peering.what: Direct private connection between two VPCs
key: Same or cross-region, same or cross-account
NOT transitive — A↔B and B↔C ≠ A↔C
Update route tables on BOTH sides
trap: Peering is not transitive — use TGW for transitive routing
Overlapping CIDRs block peering
vpc-peering.when: Connect 2-5 VPCs; use Transit Gateway for larger meshes

vpc-flow-logs.what: Capture network traffic metadata for VPC/subnet/ENI
key: Metadata only (IPs, ports, bytes, ACCEPT/REJECT) — not packet content
Publish to: CloudWatch Logs, S3, Kinesis Firehose
Query with Athena (S3) or CloudWatch Logs Insights
trap: Flow Logs=metadata; Traffic Mirroring=actual packet content
Does NOT capture: Route53 DNS queries, DHCP, instance metadata (169.254.x.x)
vpc-flow-logs.when: Troubleshoot connectivity, audit traffic, detect port scans, identify rejected connections

bastion-hosts-and-site-to-site-vpn.what: Secure access to private resources + on-prem connectivity
key: Bastion: public EC2 jump server (replace with SSM Session Manager)
Site-to-Site VPN: IPSec tunnels, VGW + CGW, 1.25 Gbps/tunnel
VPN CloudHub: multiple branches via single VGW
Accelerated VPN: over AWS Global Accelerator backbone
trap: VPN is encrypted; Direct Connect is NOT encrypted
VPN goes over public internet — latency varies; DX is dedicated private
Session Manager preferred over bastion — no port 22, no key pairs
bastion-hosts-and-site-to-site-vpn.when: VPN=quick encrypted on-prem connectivity; Bastion=legacy SSH jump (prefer SSM)

vpc-advanced-features.what: IPv6, DNS, Traffic Mirroring in VPC
key: Egress-Only IGW: IPv6 outbound only (like NAT GW but for IPv6, free)
Traffic Mirroring: copy packets to inspection tool (actual content)
IPv6: always public, no private IPv6 ranges
Resolver Inbound: on-prem DNS resolves AWS private zones
Resolver Outbound: VPC DNS forwards to on-prem
trap: IPv6 is always public — use Egress-Only IGW for private outbound IPv6
Egress-Only IGW=IPv6 only; NAT GW=IPv4 only
vpc-advanced-features.when: IPv6 dual-stack, packet inspection/forensics, hybrid DNS resolution

key: Operational Excellence: IaC, small changes, learn from failure
Security: least privilege, traceability, defense in depth
Reliability: auto-recover, horizontal scale, test failure
Performance Efficiency: right tool, serverless, go global
Cost Optimization: consumption model, right-size, commitments
Sustainability: minimize waste, use managed services
trap: Exam scenarios often map to a specific pillar — identify which one
well-architected-framework.when: Use as lens for every architecture decision

high-availability-patterns.what: Design for fault tolerance and resilience
key: Multi-AZ: ASG + ALB + RDS Multi-AZ + one NAT GW per AZ
Multi-Region: Aurora Global / DynamoDB Global Tables / S3 CRR
ASG policies: Target Tracking / Step / Scheduled / Predictive
trap: Multi-AZ=HA within region; Multi-Region=DR across regions
Scale-up has a ceiling; scale-out is theoretically unlimited

key: Large file upload: S3 Presigned URL (bypass Lambda size limits)
trap: Lambda max 15min — offload long tasks to Fargate/Batch
Large payloads: upload direct to S3 via presigned URL, not through Lambda

caching-patterns.what: Cache strategies to reduce latency and cost
key: Lazy Loading: cache-aside, cache miss=3 trips, stale possible
Write-Through: always fresh, may cache unused data
trap: DAX=DynamoDB only
ElastiCache can serve stale data — set appropriate TTL
caching-patterns.when: High read traffic, repeated queries, global low-latency requirements

cross-account-and-multi-account-patterns.what: Manage access and resources across AWS accounts
key: Cross-account role: Account A assumes role in Account B via STS
Resource-based policy: grant access directly without AssumeRole (S3, KMS, Lambda, SQS)
RAM: share VPC subnets, TGW, Route 53 rules
Control Tower: automated landing zone with guardrails
SCPs: prevent actions at account level
trap: SCPs don't apply to management account
Cross-account role needs trust on BOTH sides (role trusts account + user needs AssumeRole permission)
cross-account-and-multi-account-patterns.when: Enterprise multi-account setup, shared networking, cross-team access control

key: 4 strategies: Backup&Restore < Pilot Light < Warm Standby < Multi-Site Active-Active
trap: RPO=data loss tolerance; RTO=downtime tolerance — don't mix them up
disaster-recovery-concepts.when: Every architecture with HA/DR requirements — match strategy to RPO/RTO budget

backup-and-restore.what: Cheapest DR — backup data, rebuild on disaster
key: Tools: S3 CRR, RDS snapshot copy, EC2 AMI copy, AWS Backup
Store CFN templates in S3/CodeCommit to rebuild infra
Route 53 update DNS to new region after recovery
trap: Cheapest but slowest RTO — not for critical apps

pilot-light.what: Keep minimal core running (DB only), scale up on disaster
key: RPO: minutes (continuous DB replication); RTO: minutes-hours (launch app servers)
DB: RDS cross-region replica or Aurora Global
App servers: NOT running — launch from AMIs/CFN on failover
trap: App servers are NOT running (unlike Warm Standby where they are)
pilot-light.when: Moderate RPO/RTO requirements, low ongoing cost

warm-standby.what: Scaled-down full environment always running in DR
key: RPO: near-zero; RTO: minutes (scale up existing infra)
All components running at minimum capacity
Failover: scale ASG + promote DB + adjust Route 53 weights
trap: Warm Standby=app servers RUNNING (scaled-down); Pilot Light=app servers NOT running
warm-standby.when: Lower RTO than Pilot Light, business-critical apps, moderate budget

multi-site-active-active.what: Full capacity in multiple regions simultaneously
key: DB: Aurora Global (<1s RPO, <1min RTO) or DynamoDB Global Tables (multi-active)
Traffic: Route 53 latency/geo or Global Accelerator
Full infrastructure cost in each region
trap: Aurora Global=active-passive (one writer); DynamoDB Global=active-active (all regions writable)
Most expensive strategy
multi-site-active-active.when: Mission-critical apps, zero tolerance for downtime or data loss

dr-services-reference.what: Quick DR capability reference per service
key: RDS: snapshots cross-region, read replicas cross-region, Multi-AZ (same region)
Aurora: Global DB RPO<1s RTO<1min, Backtrack (rewind without restore)
trap: RDS cross-region read replica must be PROMOTED to become writable — severs replication
dr-services-reference.when: Reference when selecting DR approach for each service tier

key: Convertible RI: 54% off, can exchange family
Savings Plans: Compute(66% off, covers Lambda+Fargate) / EC2 Instance(72%)
trap: Savings Plans cover Lambda+Fargate; RIs do not
Convertible RI < discount than Standard RI
Capacity Reservations alone give no discount — combine with RI/SP

storage-cost-optimization.what: Reduce storage costs across S3, EBS, EFS
key: S3: Lifecycle policies to cheaper classes; delete incomplete multipart uploads
S3 Intelligent-Tiering: unpredictable access, no retrieval fee
EBS: use gp3 over gp2 (20% cheaper); delete unattached volumes
EBS: move old snapshots to Archive (75% cheaper)
trap: Standard-IA/One Zone-IA: min 30d + 128KB charge — bad for short-lived small objects
storage-cost-optimization.when: Storage is often the biggest hidden cost — always apply lifecycle policies

key: RDS Reserved Instances: up to 69% off
Aurora Serverless v2: pay for ACUs used (variable workloads)
Stop dev/test RDS when not in use (still charged for storage)
DynamoDB TTL: delete expired items free
trap: Query>Scan in DynamoDB — Scan reads entire table (expensive RCUs)
database-cost-optimization.when: Biggest DB savings: Reserved Instances for prod + stop dev instances on schedule

network-cost-optimization.what: Reduce data transfer and networking costs
key: S3→CloudFront: free; use CF to reduce direct S3 egress
S3 Gateway Endpoint: free, eliminates NAT GW costs for S3
trap: NAT GW: $0.045/hr + $0.045/GB — can be huge for high-throughput apps
Replace S3/DynamoDB traffic through NAT GW with free Gateway Endpoints
network-cost-optimization.when: Check NAT Gateway costs + cross-AZ traffic — often biggest surprise bills

cost-management-tools.what: Tools to monitor, analyze, and control AWS costs
key: Cost Explorer: visualize + forecast + rightsizing + RI/SP recommendations
CUR: most granular data → query with Athena
Compute Optimizer: ML rightsizing for EC2/Lambda/ECS/EBS
Cost Allocation Tags: track costs by project/team/env
trap: CUR=raw data; Cost Explorer=analysis; Budgets=alerts — use all three together
cost-management-tools.when: Always set up Budgets first, activate cost allocation tags from day one

key-numbers-and-limits.what: Critical limits and numbers for SAA-C03
key: S3: 5TB max object, 5GB single PUT, 100 buckets/account (soft)
SQS: 256KB msg, 14d retention, 12h visibility timeout, 20s long poll
VPC: 5 reserved IPs/subnet, /16 max CIDR, 5 VPCs/region
KMS: 4KB max direct encrypt, 7-30d delete waiting period
trap: These are the most tested numbers — memorize them
key-numbers-and-limits.when: Any scenario asking about limits, sizing, or capacity

key: Multi-AZ=HA not read scaling; Read Replicas=read scaling not HA
Direct Connect NOT encrypted by default
Lambda in VPC loses internet access
CNAME can't be at zone apex — use Alias
SCPs don't apply to management account
trap: One correct answer is usually the CHEAPER or SIMPLER option
Look for 'automatic', 'managed', 'serverless' keywords
most-common-exam-traps.when: Every exam question — check against these traps before selecting answer

key: Relational DB→RDS/Aurora; NoSQL→DynamoDB; Cache→ElastiCache; Search→OpenSearch; Warehouse→Redshift
Containers no K8s→ECS+Fargate; Containers K8s→EKS; Batch jobs→AWS Batch; Short code→Lambda
Shared Linux files→EFS; Shared Windows files→FSx for Windows; Block storage→EBS; Object storage→S3
Decouple 1:1→SQS; Fan-out→SNS; Events→EventBridge; Workflow→Step Functions; Real-time stream→Kinesis
Threat detection→GuardDuty; Vuln scan→Inspector; S3 sensitive data→Macie; Findings dashboard→Security Hub; Forensics→Detective
App secrets+rotation→Secrets Manager; Config params→SSM Parameter Store; Encryption keys→KMS; Dedicated HSM→CloudHSM
trap: If question says 'managed', 'serverless', 'no infrastructure' — lean toward managed services
If question says 'existing tool/protocol' (SFTP, Kafka, MongoDB) — lean toward compatible managed service
service-selection-cheatsheet.when: Use as first filter when reading exam scenario questions
